result:
I think I'd want everyone to have moments of genuine wonder more often - like the feeling you get when you suddenly understand something profound, or when you see something so beautiful it makes you pause, or when an idea clicks and reshapes how you see things.

Not enforced wonder or manufactured awe, but more opportunities for those authentic moments where the world feels bigger and more intricate than you realized. Where curiosity overtakes cynicism for a bit.

It seems like so many problems stem from when we stop being curious about each other and the world. When we think we already know all the answers. Wonder has this way of making us both humbler and more ambitious at the same time.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
